things.name: Bratislava Castle
things.description: Iconic four-towered castle dominating the old town above the Danube, housing the Slovak National Museum

things.name: Old Town (Stare Mesto)
things.description: Charming compact historic centre with pastel baroque buildings, quirky statues and outdoor cafes

things.name: St Martin's Cathedral
things.description: Gothic coronation cathedral where 11 Hungarian kings and queens were crowned from 1563 to 1830

things.name: Slovak National Gallery
things.description: Slovakia's main art museum in a complex of baroque and modern buildings on the Danube embankment

things.name: UFO Bridge Restaurant & Observation Deck
things.description: Panoramic viewing platform and restaurant atop the futuristic 1972 SNP Bridge over the Danube

things.name: Devín Castle
things.description: Romantic ruined castle at the confluence of the Danube and Morava rivers with views into Austria

things.name: Cumil Statue (Man at Work)
things.description: Bratislava's most photographed quirky statue — a sewage worker peeking from a manhole

things.name: Slavin War Memorial
things.description: WWII memorial and cemetery for Soviet soldiers with outstanding views over the Danube and city

things.name: Michael's Gate (Michalska brana)
things.description: The only surviving medieval gate tower (1300s) — climb to the Museum of Arms for city views

things.name: Vienna Day Trip
things.description: Austria's imperial capital is just 1 hour by boat or 75 minutes by bus — Europe's closest capital pair
